status: DECIDED — 0.5-40 Hz bandpass retained, PTB-XL+Ningbo fusion validated (3 concordant probes)
fusion: VALIDATED — combined PTB-XL+Ningbo models (no per-dataset fallback)

decision_basis.batch_effect: dataset-separation AUC ~0.95 (strong), NOT harmonizable by any bandpass (~0.05 drop). Filtering is denoising, not harmonization.
decision_basis.true_judge: cross-dataset generalization (train one dataset -> test the other), NOT the raw batch effect

M1_neurokit_clinical.note: designates 0.5-40; QRS_upslope d=-0.61 (delta signature) maximized at 0.5-40

M3_wavelet_freq.note: generalizes; gate spontaneously drops cD1/cD2 (batch-effect bands)

M7_cnn_deep.note: stronger but unstable (+/-0.13 across seeds); still generalizes

decision_basis.verdict_filter: Unanimous that filtering helps. M1 designates 0.5-40; M3 and M7 judge it equivalent to 0.5-75 (no probe reliably prefers 0.5-75). 0.5-40 retained: only positively designated + ECG standard.
decision_basis.verdict_fusion: All 3 families (clinical/frequency/deep) generalize cross-dataset despite batch effect 0.95 -> they learn WPW, not the dataset. Fusion is robust.

implications.common_filter: 0.5-40 applied identically to M1-M5, M7 and Flask (train/inference consistency)
implications.cnn_normalization: per-signal z-score for M7 (removes the inter-dataset amplitude gap at the source)
implications.nan_neurokit: extraction failure 7x more frequent in WPW (delta blurs R_Onset) -> 'extraction_failed' feature for M1; XGBoost handles NaN natively
